name: Excluded
inclusion:
1) Too little context (short file)
2) Autogenerated content
3) Original comment is in wrong language
4) Code that is commented out

name: Miscellaneous
inclusion: Anything that does not fall into any of the above categories

Code Snippet.name: Commented out code
Code Snippet.inclusion: Code that resides in a code block

Code Snippet.name: Code intended to run
Code Snippet.inclusion: Code that the model intends to run

Grammar.name: Plurality
Grammar.inclusion:
incorrect usage of plurality rules
(the subject and verb in a sentence do not agree in number. For example, "The book are on the table" should be "The book is on the table.")

Grammar.name: Gendering
Grammar.inclusion: incorrect gendering in case the language has gendered nouns

Grammar.name: Capitalization
Grammar.inclusion:
Prediction capitalizes letters that grammatically is not correct to capitalize:
e.g all capitals, every word begins with capital

Grammar.name: Cohesion
Grammar.inclusion:
1) Mistake in using a language that involves organizing words and phrases that don’t make sense (incoherence).

2) Missing (or inappropriate usage of) a comma or a quotation mark

3) Lack of local cohesion, which is logical and grammatical consistency between consecutive, adjacent sentences in paragraphs. Significant disorders of the coherence of the statement are, for example, paragraphs built from a sequence of sentences that are neither logically nor grammatically related to each other (a stream of loose thoughts, associations).

4) Syntax errors in writing refer to mistakes in the arrangement of words and phrases in a sentence that violate the rules of grammar and sentence structure
- Run-On Sentences: These happen when two or more independent clauses are joined without appropriate punctuation or conjunctions. For instance, "I like to read I also enjoy writing."
- Misplaced Modifiers: This error occurs when a word or phrase is placed too far away from the word it is meant to modify, leading to confusion or ambiguity. For example, "Running quickly, the bus was missed." This suggests that the bus was running quickly, not the person.
- Double Negatives: Using two negative words in a sentence can create confusion or ambiguity. For example, "I don't want none of that" should be "I don't want any of that."
- Lack of Parallel Structure: This occurs when a list of items in a sentence is not presented in a parallel manner. For example, "She likes hiking, to swim, and reading." This should be "She likes hiking, swimming, and reading."

Hallucination.name: Misplaced Facts
Hallucination.inclusion:
Randomly inserted facts (such as names, dates, or events) are present in the content and do not align with the context or expected content. 
For example, referencing an event that did not happen or mentioning the wrong/finctional person.

Hallucination.name: Contextual Discrepancy
Hallucination.inclusion: 1) Hallucination not grounded in the provided context.

Hallucination.name: Educated Guess
Hallucination.inclusion:
1) Syntactically correct but semantically or factually incorrect
2) Grounded in the provided context.

Incorrect comment format.name: Style Inconsistency
Incorrect comment format.inclusion:
1) Model uses outdated format of javadoc
2) Model uses comment format that is inconsistent with the standards
3) Model repeated auto-generated-comment like format which is not informative enough, instead of generating an actual description
4) Model does not follow the javadoc format that is present in the rest of the file (if present format is correct)

Incorrect comment format.name: Omitted Identifier
Incorrect comment format.inclusion:
1) Model starts enlisting @params, but misses some of them
2) Generation started with a tag @return but then doesn't have @params
3) Generated @params, but does not have @return for a method that does not return void

Linguistic.name: Linguistic Error
Linguistic.inclusion: Linguistic errors are all errors related to the linguistic content of the generated text

Linguistic.name: Grammar
Linguistic.inclusion: Language is correct, grammatical mistake was made.
Linguistic.exclusion: Exclude errors that are related to stylistic choices rather than grammatical correctness (e.g., passive vs. active voice preferences, unless clearly incorrect or unsuitable for certain languages).

Linguistic.name: Usage of incorrect synonym
Linguistic.inclusion:
Usage of a similar word with an incorrect meaning in context
(e.g. home->house)
Linguistic.exclusion: Exclude synonyms that are contextually appropriate, even if they are not the most precise term.

Linguistic.name: Wrong language
Linguistic.inclusion: The model predicts a comment (or significant part of it) in a language other than the target language

Memorization.name: Contains PII
Memorization.description: Personally identifiable information (PII) is information that, when used alone or with other relevant data, can identify an individual.
Memorization.inclusion: Personally identifiable information is included in the generated comment (fictional or did not occur in the original prompt)
Memorization.exclusion: Exclude non-specific information that cannot be used to identify an individual (e.g., generic job titles, generic first names in a certain language).

Memorization.name: Contains URL
Memorization.inclusion: URL for a file or repository is included

Memorization.name: Verbatim Memorization
Memorization.inclusion:
1) The model memorized the content verbatim 
2) the text would not be generated if not for memorization

Model-specific.name: Model Specific
Model-specific.inclusion: Model specific errors are all errors which are related to the workings of LLMs

Model-specific.name: Incoherent Generation
Model-specific.inclusion: Model outputs random words which have no logic between them

Model-specific.name: Copy Context
Model-specific.inclusion: Model copies the surrounding context verbatim

Model-specific.name: Memorization
Model-specific.inclusion: Model recognised the code to some capacity

Model-specific.name: Early Termination
Model-specific.inclusion: Model stops generating in the middle of prediction, while the comment is clearly not complete or did not generate anything

Model-specific.name: Late Termination
Model-specific.inclusion:
The comment continues producing content even though it should have stopped earlier. 
e.g 
1. When it includes unnecessary empty tags (@version)
2. Continues adding line comments even though it is unnecessary

Model-specific.name: Repetition
Model-specific.inclusion: Model generates and repeats what it has already said it in some capacity

Repetition.name: Pattern repetition
Repetition.inclusion: Model generated a repeating pattern: eg. 1,2,3,4,5,6,

Repetition.name: Verbatim repetition
Repetition.inclusion: Model generated verbatim repetition: eg. i am repeating i am repeating i am repeating

Semantic.name: Semantic error
Semantic.inclusion: Semantic errors are all errors related to the semantics or meaning of the generated content

Semantic.name: Missing details
Semantic.inclusion:
1) Description does not fully describe the content of the summarized code. 
2) Current information does not describe the full functionality of code being summarized. 
3) Current information does not describe the entire purpose of the summarized code.
4) Generated comment is too generic
Semantic.exclusion:
1) Exclude high-level summaries that align with the expected response format
2) Exclude comments that are general but still provide sufficient understanding of the code's functionality, especially in simple or trivial code segments.

Semantic.name: Too specific
Semantic.inclusion: Generated comment is too specific. Includes detailed summary of every line, which defeats the point of documentation and summarization

Semantic.name: Hallucination
Semantic.inclusion: Category for hallucination generations, i.e. factually incorrect or not related to input prompt

Semantic.name: Code snippet inclusion
Semantic.inclusion: Model generates actual code outside of comment
Semantic.exclusion:
1) Exclude comments that include minimal code snippets for clarity or example purposes
2) Exclude short generated code snippets that are created for completion and they fit into the program well

Syntax.name: Syntax
Syntax.inclusion: Syntax errors are all errors which are related to the syntax of the comments

Syntax.name: Incorrect comment format
Syntax.inclusion:
1) Model uses outdated format of javadoc
2) Model uses comment format that is inconsistent with the standards
3) Errors with javadoc format

Wrong language.name: Undesired translations
Wrong language.inclusion: Translations that are correct but undesired in the language because the words are seldomly used in that context
Wrong language.exclusion: Exclude correct use of foreign words that are commonly integrated into the language being used

Wrong language.name: Incorrect language
Wrong language.inclusion: The model predicts a comment (or significant part of it) in a language other than the target language
